plan_metrics.plan_content:
## Summary

Here's the full implementation plan for the `image-text-to-text` pipeline:

### What I found in the codebase

- **`AutoModelForImageTextToText`** already exists in `src/models.js` (line 8736) with 11 registered vision-language models (LLaVA, SmolVLM, PaliGemma, Qwen2-VL, Idefics3, Florence2, Moondream1, etc.)
- **No pipeline** currently exposes this via the `pipeline()` API â€” that's the gap to fill
- **Only `src/pipelines.js`** needs to change (no other files)

### 5 edits to `src/pipelines.js`

| # | What | Where |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Add `AutoModelForImageTextToText` to the `import { ... } from './models.js'` block | Lines 21â€“45 |
| 2 | Add JSDoc typedefs: `ImageTextToTextSingle`, `ImageTextToTextOutput`, `ImageTextToTextPipelineCallback`, `ImageTextToTextPipelineType` | After `ImageToTextPipeline` ends (~line 2005) |
| 3 | Add `export class ImageTextToTextPipeline extends Pipeline` with `_call(images, text, generate_kwargs={})` | Right after the typedefs |
| 4 | Add `"image-text-to-text"` entry to `SUPPORTED_TASKS` with `AutoModelForImageTextToText`, `AutoProcessor`, `AutoTokenizer` | After `"image-to-text"` entry (~line 3255) |
| 5 | Add `"image-text-to-text"` bullet to the `pipeline()` JSDoc | ~line 3427 |

### Key design decisions
- `_call` reuses the existing `prepareImages()` helper for input normalization
- `this.processor(preparedImages, text)` â€” the AutoProcessor for these VLMs handles both image + text together, returning `{ pixel_values, input_ids, attention_mask, ... }`
- `this.model.generate({ ...inputs, ...generate_kwargs })` â€” spreads all processor outputs; no pixel_values loop needed (unlike encoder-decoder Vision2Seq)
- Return shape mirrors `ImageToTextPipeline`: single â†’ `[{generated_text}]`, batched â†’ `[[{generated_text}], ...]`

details.command: node -e "const p = require('./src/pipelines.js'); if (typeof p.ImageTextToTextPipeline !== 'function') { process.exit(1); } console.log('OK')"
